_meta.notes: MK master locale skeleton for WPA partnerships page

topbar.line_1: founder-led partnership architecture for institutions, collaborators and strategic infrastructure allies.

hero.eyebrow: Institutional Collaboration • Strategic Infrastructure • Recurring Value
hero.title: 17+3 partnership architecture for a world-class, automated and founder-led WPA ecosystem.
hero.subtitle: World Protocol Academy is designed to grow through structured institutional collaboration, not random networking.

doctrine.label: Partnership Doctrine
doctrine.title: WPA does not collect logos. It builds structured value relationships.
doctrine.lead: Every collaborator in WPA must fit a clear function: authority, distribution, training delivery, institutional access, academic legitimacy, infrastructure support or recurring value.

box_1.title: What a WPA partner must bring
box_1.text: Credibility, reach, institutional access, academic seriousness, implementation value, delivery capacity or infrastructure support.

box_2.title: What WPA must bring in return
box_2.text: Structured programmes, certificate pathways, protocol intelligence, founder-backed authority, publications, AI-supported tools, institutional training formats and a serious professional identity framework.

core17.label: 17 Core Institutional Collaborator Types
core17.title: The core institutional ecosystem of World Protocol Academy.
core17.lead: These are not yet specific names. They are the 17 strategic categories through which WPA can expand globally with coherence, seriousness and long-term value.

items.title: Universities
items.text: Academic collaborators for lectures, curriculum integration, guest modules, certificate alignment and scholarly legitimacy.

items.title: Diplomatic Academies
items.text: Natural partners for diplomacy, protocol, statecraft and executive readiness learning pathways.

items.title: Ministries of Foreign Affairs
items.text: High-value institutional partners for diplomatic protocol, official visits, executive briefings and formal readiness.

items.title: Embassies and Diplomatic Missions
items.text: Operational partners for briefings, diplomatic communication, event preparation and protocol alignment.

items.title: Public Administration Training Centres
items.text: Partners for state, municipal and civil-service training in protocol, communication and institutional conduct.

items.title: Cabinet and Protocol Units
items.text: Direct applied collaborators for high-level event management, official visits, speaking order and representation discipline.

items.title: Defence and Military Academies
items.text: Partners for military protocol, ceremonial discipline, representational order and defence-facing readiness.

items.title: Police and Security Academies
items.text: Partners for security-aware formal conduct, movement discipline, public events and institutional sensitivity.

items.title: Municipalities and Executive Offices
items.text: Local-level collaborators for protocol literacy, public representation and event hosting competence.

items.title: Business Chambers and Associations
items.text: Partners for executive presence, business protocol, representation and conference-facing professionalism.

items.title: Conference and Event Organisations
items.text: Natural partners for real-world application, workshop placement, event standards and protocol design.

items.title: Hospitality and Service Institutes
items.text: Partners for hosting, service etiquette, reception logic and ceremonial hospitality training.

items.title: PR and Communication Institutes
items.text: Collaborators for media conduct, institutional messaging, official communication and public presence programmes.

items.title: Media and Journalism Schools
items.text: Partners for public speech, interview conduct, camera presence and institutional communication readiness.

items.title: Language Centres and English Partners
items.text: Partners for the WPA Professional English layer across protocol, diplomacy, institutional email and conference communication.

items.title: Libraries, Archives and Knowledge Institutions
items.text: Partners for bibliographic depth, archive access, reference strength and academic seriousness.

items.title: Think Tanks and Research Centres
items.text: Collaborators for policy-oriented work, doctrine development, papers, briefings and intellectual authority.

plus3.label: +3 Strategic Infrastructure Layers
plus3.title: The automation and governance layers that make WPA scalable.
plus3.lead: These three are not ordinary partners. They are strategic infrastructure allies that help WPA operate as a world-class automated academy.

routes.label: Partnership Routes
routes.title: Not all partners relate to WPA in the same way.
routes.lead: WPA should work with different partner types through different routes: academic, institutional, delivery, distribution, prestige and infrastructure.

revenue.label: Recurring and Passive Value Logic
revenue.title: How partnerships can strengthen recurring income without cheapening WPA.
revenue.lead: The goal is not random monetization. The goal is dignified, scalable recurring value rooted in real training, certification and infrastructure.

cta.title: The WPA partnership system should feel serious, not noisy.
cta.text: The right institutional, academic and infrastructure relationships can turn WPA into a stable, global and automated academy without losing dignity or intellectual control.

footer.brand_text: 17+3 partnership architecture of World Protocol Academy: institutional collaborators, strategic infrastructure partners, certification logic, AI support and recurring value pathways.
footer.disclaimer: WPA partnerships should strengthen authority, delivery and recurring value — not dilute the Academy with symbolic noise.
